posting.sourceTitle: International Union of Operating Engineers Local 158 Training Fund District # 106
posting.sponsorName: International Union of Operating Engineers (IUOE) Local Union #158 Training Fund District 106
posting.locationSummary: Capital District, Hudson Valley, Mohawk Valley, North Country, and Southern Tier regions
posting.applicationSummary: Apply in person at 44 Hannay Lane, Glenmont, NY 12077, Monday through Friday from 7:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. for 10 business days or until 50 applications are issued. Applications must be completed on the premises.
posting.applicationHours: 7:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., Monday through Friday, for 10 business days
posting.applicationLimitDetails: Only 50 applications will be distributed, on a first-come, first-served basis, and recruitment ends when 50 applications have been issued or after 10 business days, whichever comes first.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 18 years old.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED. Proof is required at the time of acceptance into the program.
posting.residencyRequirement: Must live within the geographic jurisdiction of Local Union #106 for at least six months before this recruitment. Eligible counties include Albany, Broome, Chenango, Clinton, Columbia, Dutchess, Essex, Franklin, Fulton, Greene, Hamilton, Herkimer, Montgomery, Otsego, Rensselaer, Saratoga, Schenectady, Schoharie, Tioga, Warren, and Washington counties.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation to and from various job sites in Local Union #106’s jurisdiction and required classes at the sponsor’s training center. Must possess a valid driver’s license to operate company vehicles.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a drug/alcohol screening at the sponsor’s expense after selection and before enrollment. May be subject to random drug testing at any time during apprenticeship.
posting.classRequirement: Must attend required classes at the sponsor’s training center.
allRequirements: Must be at least 18 years old.
Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED. Proof is required at the time of acceptance into the program.
Must be able to read, understand and hear verbal instructions and warnings given in English.
Must have a minimum of one year of trade-related experience, or have completed a construction-related pre-apprenticeship, or have successfully completed a construction-related training program.

Must be physically able to perform the work as determined by signed affidavit, including lifting heavy objects, working in confined spaces, working with and/or operating hazardous machinery, working in extreme dust conditions, extreme heat, cold or damp weather, working over water and from heights, and being exposed to loud noises or respiratory irritants.

Must live within the geographic jurisdiction of Local Union #106 for at least six months prior to this recruitment. This includes Albany, Broome, Chenango, Clinton, Columbia, Dutchess, Essex, Franklin, Fulton, Greene, Hamilton, Herkimer, Montgomery, Otsego, Rensselaer, Saratoga, Schenectady, Schoharie, Tioga, Warren, and Washington counties.
Must have reliable transportation to and from various job sites in Local Union #106’s jurisdiction and required classes at sponsor’s training center.
Must pass a drug/alcohol screening, at the expense of the sponsor, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship. May be subject to random drug testing at any time during apprenticeship.
Must possess a valid driver’s license to operate company vehicles. Proof required at the time of acceptance.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 18, have a high school diploma or equivalent, have English reading/listening ability, meet trade experience or pre-apprenticeship/training requirements, live in the union’s jurisdiction for six months, have reliable transportation and a valid driver’s license, pass a drug/alcohol screening, and be physically able to do the work.
posting.contactName: JATC for Operating Engineers of Albany, Local Union #106
posting.contactSummary: Contact JATC for Operating Engineers of Albany, Local Union #106 at (315) 299-6104 for further information.
